key_insight: Scaling up GPT-style language models delays, but does not eliminate, plasticity loss during long training, even in multilingual and stationary settings.
engineering_relevance: For continual-learning and adaptive NLP systems, parameter scale alone is not a sufficient safeguard against eventual degradation in the ability to learn new information.
method: Train GPT-style Transformer models from 5M to 314M non-embedding parameters
Use a multilingual continual-learning setup with a held-out Vietnamese probing task
Compare plasticity under both abrupt task shifts and stationary multilingual training
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a measurable deterioration on the held-out Vietnamese probe across model sizes, with onset following a sublinear scaling law.
watch_item: Larger models may postpone plasticity loss, but relying only on scale can still leave long-running deployments vulnerable to adaptation failure over time.
paper_title: Can Scale Save Us From Plasticity Loss in Large Language Models?
